description: Baseline (unmodified train.py)
Changed only the learning rate from 2e-4 to 1e-4 to reduce over-updating on a mostly QA instruction dataset and improve validation loss stability under the short time budget.

Changed only the LoRA target modules to include MLP projections (`gate_proj`, `up_proj`, `down_proj`) in addition to attention, aiming to increase adaptation capacity for the dominant financial QA task without changing optimization dynamics.
Changed only the LoRA rank from 16 to 32 to increase adaptation capacity for the dominant financial QA task while keeping the successful target-module setup unchanged.
Changed only the learning rate from 2e-4 to 3e-4 to speed adaptation within the 300-second budget, since the lower LR underfit while expanded LoRA capacity already improved validation loss.
Changed only the LoRA dropout from 0.05 to 0.0 to remove regularization that may be hurting adaptation under the very short 300-second budget and mostly homogeneous financial QA distribution.
Changed only the learning rate from 3e-4 to 4e-4 to test whether slightly faster adaptation improves validation loss within the strict 300-second budget on the mostly financial-QA dataset.

Changed only the learning rate from 4e-4 to 5e-4 to test whether even faster adaptation further improves validation loss within the strict 300-second budget after consistent gains from successive LR increases.
Changed only the warmup ratio from 0.03 to 0.0 because the 300-second budget and steadily improving higher learning rates suggest warmup is wasting too much of the limited optimization window.
Changed only the learning-rate scheduler from cosine to constant so the model can sustain the proven strong 4e-4 learning rate throughout the short 300-second run instead of decaying too early.

Changed only the evaluation frequency from every 50 steps to every 100 steps to reduce evaluation overhead and allow more training updates within the fixed 300-second budget, which may lower val_loss on this short-run setup.

Changed only `num_train_epochs` from 1 to 2 so the trainer can use more of the fixed 300-second budget for additional optimization steps, since recent gains came from reducing overhead and increasing effective updates.

Changed only `max_length` from 1024 to 768 to reduce per-step compute and fit more optimization steps within the 300-second budget, which is likely beneficial for this mostly short-form financial QA dataset.

Changed only `gradient_accumulation_steps` from 4 to 2 to increase optimizer update frequency within the fixed 300-second budget, aiming to improve adaptation on the mostly short financial-QA dataset without increasing VRAM much.

Changed only the optimizer from `adamw_torch` to `paged_adamw_8bit` to reduce optimizer-state overhead and likely fit more efficient training within the 300-second budget without changing the proven optimization schedule.

Changed only the learning rate from 4e-4 to 3.5e-4 to test whether a slightly lower peak LR improves generalization after 4e-4 helped but 5e-4 began to overstep, potentially reducing val_loss further within the same short budget.

Changed only the weight decay from 0.01 to 0.0 because the short-budget, low-dropout QLoRA setup on a mostly homogeneous financial QA dataset likely benefits from less regularization and slightly faster fitting.
Changed only the per-device batch size from 4 to 8 to increase tokens processed per optimizer step and improve gradient quality within the 24GB A10G budget at max_length 768.
Changed only the LoRA rank from 16 to 8 to test whether a smaller adapter improves generalization and training efficiency under the 300-second budget after larger capacity underperformed.

Changed only `lora_alpha` from 32 to 16 to test whether a lower LoRA scaling factor improves generalization and stabilizes updates at the current strong 3.5e-4 learning rate under the short 300-second budget.
Changed only the learning rate from 3.5e-4 to 3.25e-4 to test whether a slightly gentler step size improves generalization after 3.5e-4 narrowly beat 4e-4 and nearby higher rates began to overstep.

Changed only the LoRA dropout from 0.0 to 0.05 to add a small amount of regularization, which may improve validation loss now that batch size and learning rate are already near-optimal on this mostly homogeneous financial-QA dataset.
Changed only `eval_steps` from 100 to 200 to further reduce evaluation overhead and fit more training updates into the 300-second budget, since less frequent eval already improved validation loss.
